  • [ - ] BeautifulLoser - member since 30-Dec-12

    @Yman In matches like these with this amount of quality in midfield you will always have to concede in the channels near the sidelines. So, the target will be either Raum or Kehrer. Both Süle and Rüdiger must be perfectly conscious about that. Else they should quit their jobs immediately. It is absolutely no excuse that Kehrer's side was open. It was expected to be open. In this case it was particularly open because Gnabry pushed up too high and didn't care about Olmo. Kehrer did a respectable job with 3 interceptions and 6 tackles in his area of responsibility. In this situation Süle had to control Morata which he should be perfectly capable of doing. Morata! Not Mbappé or Lewandowski or freaking Karim Benzema. Morata has an extremely restricted set of skills, and the run he made was predictable AF. Süle did just blackout: he trotted 10 meters just staring at the ball while being completely unaware of Morata's position. Since he left Bayern to join BVB, he grew fat, slow, and lazy.

    3 years ago

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    I don't know who will win (it can even be a tie), but it will be a high scoring game for sure! How do I know this? Flick is not the type of coach to commit the same mistake twice: he learned from the Japanese fiasco and now will rally his troops from the very first minute. At any rate, if you don't believe me, you will believe the starting lineup: if you see Sane, Gnabry, Coman, Muller, Goretzka, Musiala, and Kimmich all start at the same time, you will know that an attacking game will unfold instead of this garbage we have seen all day long. Spain, however, has the attacking firepower to counterattack Germany, provided that they will play the same 11 who played Costa Rica. Looking forward to this game, which might as well would qualify for the FINAL of this World Cup!
